description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, crescent-shaped blades on either side of a central mounting, both with slightly worn and textured surfaces, suggesting heavy use or a rugged finish. The handle is long, straight, and appears to be made of dark metal, ending in a pointed pommel for added balance or secondary use. The central mounting of the blades is reinforced with metallic bands and possibly decorative elements, giving the axe a medieval or fantasy aesthetic. The overall color palette is dominated by dark metallic tones with hints of brown or bronze at the blade mount. This axe would be visually striking and intimidating if found in an urban environment, such as a themed park, display, or as a prop in a garage or workshop.
description: A double-headed battle axe with large, dark crescent blades and a long, straight metal handle, featuring rugged textures and reinforced mounting.
description_view_0: The axe is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the double blades and the straight handle.
description_view_1: The full face of the double-headed axe is visible, highlighting the wide, symmetrical blades and reinforced central mount.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, mirroring view 0, with the handle and blade edges aligned in a narrow silhouette.
description_view_3: The opposite face of the axe is shown, similar to view 1, displaying the broad blades and central mounting details.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, cylindrical metal handle with a pointed pommel, likely steel or iron, no visible grip texture, cross-section appears round.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with symmetrical edges, no beard, reinforced central mount, rugged and slightly worn surface.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible reinforcement at the blade-handle junction.
